creator: bloomfire.com

evidence.text: Knowledge sharing is a behavior: the act of exchanging information, expertise, and experience between people. Knowledge management is the broader organizational discipline that creates the systems, processes, governance, and culture that enable knowledge sharing to happen consistently and at scale. Knowledge management is the infrastructure; knowledge sharing is what flows through it.
